headerDisplay: Native TypeScript support for legacy **TC-series** (Tend/Seedonk) myQ garage cameras. Streams video, camera audio, and HomeKit push-to-talk without a phone or Python runtime. Newer WebRTC/KVS myQ cameras are not supported.
footerDisplay: Use a Homebridge child bridge for camera isolation. Run `homebridge-myq-camera-doctor --live 30` from the Homebridge terminal to validate credentials, FFmpeg, signaling, and media.

tokenFile.title: OAuth token file override
tokenFile.description: Optional. Leave blank to use Homebridge storage: <storage>/myq-camera/token.json. The plugin rotates this token file atomically.

ffmpeg.title: Custom FFmpeg path
ffmpeg.description: Leave blank to use Homebridge's bundled FFmpeg. A custom build needs libx264, libopus, and libfdk_aac for talkback.

talkback.title: HomeKit push-to-talk
talkback.description: Routes HomeKit return audio into the same native camera session.

copyVideo.title: Copy native H.264
copyVideo.description: Avoids video transcoding but advertises only native 1280×720 at 20 fps. Leave off for maximum HomeKit compatibility.

keepAliveSeconds.title: Session keep-alive (seconds)
keepAliveSeconds.description: How long the shared camera session stays warm after the last snapshot/stream finishes, so a follow-up request reuses it instantly. Snapshots and live streams share this one session, which is why they no longer collide and multiple viewers can watch at once.

maxViewers.title: Max concurrent viewers
maxViewers.description: How many HomeKit clients may stream at once (they all share one camera session). 0 = unlimited (capped at 8, since HomeKit needs a concrete number).

snapshotRefreshInterval.title: Snapshot refresh interval (seconds)
snapshotRefreshInterval.description: While the shared session is open (streaming or in the warm keep-alive window), snapshot requests wait for a fresh still decoded from the latest warm keyframe without holding the session open. When the session is closed, a request opens one to refresh a still older than this many seconds (then keeps it warm). 0 = refresh on every snapshot request.

snapshotTimeoutSeconds.title: Snapshot timeout (seconds)
snapshotTimeoutSeconds.description: Maximum time a fresh HomeKit snapshot request may wait for the shared camera session and one decodable frame before returning an error or the last cached still.

streamStartupTimeoutSeconds.title: Stream startup timeout (seconds)
streamStartupTimeoutSeconds.description: Maximum time HomeKit stream startup may wait for the shared camera session, hole-punch retries, and FFmpeg startup.

videoPunchTimeoutSeconds.title: Video hole-punch timeout (seconds)
videoPunchTimeoutSeconds.description: Per-attempt timeout for the native Seedonk video hole punch.

videoPunchAttempts.title: Video hole-punch attempts
videoPunchAttempts.description: How many native Seedonk video hole-punch attempts to try before failing a camera session open.

videoPunchRetryDelaySeconds.title: Video hole-punch retry delay (seconds)
videoPunchRetryDelaySeconds.description: Delay between failed video hole-punch attempts.

interSessionCooldownSeconds.title: Inter-session cooldown (seconds)
interSessionCooldownSeconds.description: Minimum gap after closing a native myQ media session before opening the next one, giving the camera time to release its single-viewer slot.

deviceId.title: Camera device ID or myQ alias
deviceId.description: Leave blank to use the first TC-series camera on the account.
